eventDescription.latestDescription:
The SSL/TLS certificate for your Amazon RDS DB instance is due for rotation. AWS is updating the Certificate Authority (CA) from rds-ca-2019 to rds-ca-rsa2048-g1 to improve security. The current certificate will expire and must be rotated before February 28, 2026.

Affected DB instance:
  - DB Identifier: dndn-rds-primary
  - Region: ap-northeast-2
  - Current CA: rds-ca-2019
  - New CA: rds-ca-rsa2048-g1
  - Certificate expiry: February 28, 2026

To apply the new certificate without downtime:
1. Download the new CA bundle: https://truststore.pki.rds.amazonaws.com/global/global-bundle.pem
2. Update your application's trust store with the new CA certificate.
3. Modify the DB instance to apply the certificate rotation (this will cause a brief reboot).

If your application enforces SSL certificate verification and is not updated before the expiry date, connections to the database will fail.

For step-by-step instructions: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/UsingWithRDS.SSL-certificate-rotation.html
